title: Fearless and auspicious path | HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa | SB 6.1.17 | 05-12-2019
speaker: HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

segments.text: Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 6, Chapter 1, and Text 17.
Translation, purported by His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Śrī Prabhupāda.
Translation, the path followed by pure devotees who are well-behaved and fully endowed with the best qualifications
is certainly the most auspicious path in this material world.
It is free from fear and it is authorized by the Shastras.
Purported by Śrī Prabhupāda, one should not think that the person who takes to Bhakti
is one who cannot perform the ritualistic ceremonies
One should not take to Bhakti in the Karmakanda section of the Vedas
or is not sufficiently educated to speculate on spiritual subjects.
Māyāvādīs generally allege that the Bhakti path is for women and illiterates.
The Bhakti path is followed by the most learned scholars such as the Gosvāmīs,
These are the actual followers of the Bhakti path.
Whether or not one is educated or aristocratic, one must follow in their footsteps.
One must follow the path of the Mahājanas.
The Mahājanas are those who are taken to the path of devotional service.
For these great personalities are the perfect persons.
One who has unflinching devotion to the Personality of Godhead
has all the good qualities of the demigods.
The less intelligent, however, misunderstand the Bhakti path
and therefore allege that it is for one who cannot execute ritualistic ceremonies or speculate.
As confirmed here by the word sadrīcīnaḥ,
Bhakti is the path that is appropriate,
not the paths of karmakāṇḍa and jñānakāṇḍa.
Māyāvādīs may be sūśīlā, sādava, well-behaved saintly persons,
but there is nevertheless some doubt about whether they are actually making progress,
for they have not accepted the path of Bhakti.
On the other hand, those who follow the path of the Āchāryas are sūśīlā and sādavaha,
but furthermore their path is akutho bhaya, which means free from fear.
One should fearlessly follow the twelve Mahājanas
and their line of disciplic succession
and thus be liberated from the clutches of Māyā.
That is the sense of the Bhaktivedanta purport.
